name: U.S. federal sex designation policy
status_summary: Federal executive orders issued in January 2025 removed the X sex designation from Social Security records, U.S. passports, and U.S. passport cards, and replaced the self-certification policy that had been in effect since 2022 with a requirement for physician documentation or a court order for M-to-F or F-to-M changes. Active litigation may alter this. Until that changes, federal sex designation changes are not available through the routes that were available before January 2025.

references.label: National Center for Transgender Equality: Federal ID Updates
